Informative.desc: Show this help message or help for a specific command
List search paths for config files
List search paths for presets and logos
List the supported features that fastfetch was compiled with
Keep Fastfetch running and refresh the output every <num> milliseconds
Informative.remark: Set to 0 (default) to disable this behavior; does not work with --json

Config.desc: Specify the config file or preset to load
Config.remark: The file is searched in the order shown by "fastfetch --list-config-paths". Use "-" to read config from stdin, or "none" to disable further config loading. See also https://github.com/fastfetch-cli/fastfetch/wiki/Configuration for details

Config.desc: Generate a minimal config file at the specified path
Config.remark: Defaults to "~/.config/fastfetch/config.jsonc". Prints the generated config if <path> is "-"

Config.desc: Generate a full config file with all optional settings at the specified path
Config.remark: Defaults to "~/.config/fastfetch/config.jsonc". Prints the generated config if <path> is "-"

Config.desc: Generate a config file at the specified path, overwriting any existing file

General.desc: Use separate threads for HTTP requests
Set the timeout (ms) for WMI queries
Set the timeout (ms) when waiting for child processes
Specify whether display detection should use DRM only
remark: Use this option if you encounter problems with other detection methods.

enum.true: Try `libdrm` first, then `sysfs` if libdrm fails

General.desc: Specify whether to detect and display versions for terminal, shell, editor, and others

Logo.desc: Set the logo source. Use "none" to disable logo output
Logo.remark: Use either a built-in logo name or a path to an image file. See also https://github.com/fastfetch-cli/fastfetch/wiki/Logo-options

Logo.desc: Set the type of logo specified by "--logo"

enum.auto: If a value is provided, try built-in first, then file. Otherwise auto-detect the logo
enum.file: Text file, printed with color code replacement
enum.data: Text data, printed with color code replacement
enum.kitty: Image file, rendered using the Kitty graphics protocol
enum.kitty-direct: Image file, tells the terminal emulator to read image data from the specified file
enum.kitty-icat: Image file, displayed using `kitten icat`. Requires the `kitten` binary
enum.iterm: Image file, rendered using the iTerm graphics protocol
enum.chafa: Image file, printed as ASCII art using libchafa
enum.raw: Image file, printed as raw binary string

Logo.desc: Set the width of the logo (in characters) if it is an image
Set the height of the logo (in characters) if it is an image
Specify whether the logo should fill the specified width and height as much as possible without stretching
Logo.remark: Supported by iTerm image protocol only

Logo.desc: Override a color in the logo
Logo.remark: See `-h color` for the list of available colors

Logo.desc: Set the padding on the left and right sides of the logo
Set the padding on the left side of the logo
Set the padding on the right side of the logo
Set the padding at the top of the logo
Specify whether to print remaining logo lines when the logo is taller than the module list
Set the display position of the logo
If true, regenerate the image logo cache
Short for --logo-type file --logo <path>
Short for --logo-type file-raw --logo <path>
Short for --logo-type data --logo <data>
Short for --logo-type data-raw --logo <data>
Short for --logo-type raw --logo <path>
Short for --logo-type sixel --logo <path>
Short for --logo-type kitty --logo <path>
Short for --logo-type kitty-direct --logo <path>
Short for --logo-type kitty-icat --logo <path>
Short for --logo-type iterm --logo <path>
Short for --logo-type chafa --logo <path>
Produce character-cell output using foreground colors only
Logo.remark: See the Chafa documentation for details

Logo.desc: Specify character symbols to employ in final output
Logo.remark: See the Chafa documentation for details

Logo.desc: Determine how colors are used in the output
Logo.remark: This value maps to the integer value of enum ChafaCanvasMode. See the Chafa documentation for details

enum.INDEXED_240: 256 colors, but avoid using the lower 16 whose values vary between terminal environments
enum.INDEXED_16: 16 colors using the aixterm ANSI extension
enum.FGBG_BGFG: Default foreground and background colors, plus inversion
enum.FGBG: Default foreground and background colors. No ANSI codes will be used
enum.INDEXED_8: 8 colors, compatible with original ANSI X3.64
enum.INDEXED_16_8: 16 FG colors (8 of which enabled with bold/bright) and 8 BG colors

Logo.desc: Set the color space used for quantization
Logo.remark: This value maps to the integer value of enum ChafaColorSpace. See the Chafa documentation for details

enum.RGB: RGB color space. Fast but imprecise
enum.DIN99D: DIN99d color space. Slower, but good perceptual color precision

Logo.desc: Set the output dithering mode (no effect with 24-bit color)
Logo.remark: This value maps to the integer value of enum ChafaDitherMode. See the Chafa documentation for details

Display.desc: Set the structure of the fetch
Display.remark: Must be a colon-separated list of module keys. Use "fastfetch --list-modules" to see available options

Display.desc: Disable specific modules in the structure
Display.remark: Must be a colon-separated list of keys

Display.desc: Show execution time (ms) for individual modules
Disable colors
Display.remark: Auto-detected based on isatty(1) by default

Display.desc: Set the color of both keys and title
Set the color of the keys
Display.remark: Doesn't affect Title, Separator, and Colors modules. See `-h color` for the list of available colors

Display.desc: Set the color of the title
Display.remark: See `-h color` for the list of available colors

Display.desc: Set the color of module output
Display.remark: See `-h color` for the list of available colors

Display.desc: Set the color of the key-value separator
Display.remark: See `-h color` for the list of available colors

Display.desc: Specify whether to abbreviate duration values
Display.remark: If true, output is shown as "1h 2m" instead of "1 hour, 2 mins"

Display.desc: Specify whether to put a space before the unit in duration values

enum.default: Use the default behavior of the module
enum.always: Always put a space before the unit
enum.never: Never put a space before the unit

Display.desc: Align the width of keys to <num> characters
Set the left padding of keys to <num> characters
Specify whether to show an icon before string keys

enum.icon: Show icon (requires a recent Nerd Font)
enum.both: Show both icon and string (alias of `both-1`)
enum.both-0: Show both icon and string with no spaces between them
enum.both-1: Show both icon and string with a space between them
enum.both-2: Show both icon and string with 2 spaces between them
enum.both-3: Show both icon and string with 3 spaces between them
enum.both-4: Show both icon and string with 4 spaces between them

Display.desc: Specify whether keys, title, and ASCII logo should be printed in bright color
Set the separator between key and value
Print errors when they occur
Specify whether to disable line wrap during execution
Specify whether to hide the cursor during execution
Set the percentage output style
Set the number of decimal places to use when formatting percentages
Set color used for the green state of percentage bars and numbers
Display.remark: See `-h color` for the list of available colors

Display.desc: Set color used for the yellow state of percentage bars and numbers
Display.remark: See `-h color` for the list of available colors

Display.desc: Set color used for the red state of percentage bars and numbers
Display.remark: See `-h color` for the list of available colors

Display.desc: Specify whether to put a space before the percentage symbol

enum.default: Use the default behavior of the module
enum.always: Always put a space before the unit
enum.never: Never put a space before the unit

Display.desc: Specify the width of the percentage number, in characters
Display.remark: This option affects only percentage numbers, not bars

Display.desc: Set the character used for the elapsed part of percentage bars
Set the character used for the total part of percentage bars
Set the string to use at the left border of percentage bars
Set the string to use at the right border of percentage bars
If both bar-border-left-elapsed and bar-border-right-elapsed are set, the border is used as part of the bar content
If both bar-border-left-elapsed and bar-border-right-elapsed are set, the border is used as part of the bar content
Set the color used for the elapsed part of percentage bars
Display.remark: By default, this is auto-selected by percent-color-{green,yellow,red}

Display.desc: Set the color used for the total part of percentage bars
Set the color used for percentage bar borders
Set the width of percentage bars in characters
Specify whether stdout buffering should be disabled
Set the number of decimal places to use when formatting sizes
Set the binary prefix to use when formatting sizes
Set the largest binary prefix to use when formatting sizes
Specify whether to put a space before the unit

enum.default: Use the default behavior of the module
enum.always: Always put a space before the unit
enum.never: Never put a space before the unit

Display.desc: Set the number of decimal places to use when printing CPU/GPU frequency in GHz
Specify whether to put a space before the unit

enum.default: Use the default behavior of the module
enum.always: Always put a space before the unit
enum.never: Never put a space before the unit

Display.desc: Set the number of decimal places to use when printing fractional values
Display.remark: If negative, the number of digits will be automatically determined based on the value

Display.desc: Set when to keep trailing zeros
Set the temperature unit
Set the number of decimal places to use when printing temperatures
Set color used for the green state of temperature values
Display.remark: See `-h color` for the list of available colors

Display.desc: Set color used for the yellow state of temperature values
Display.remark: See `-h color` for the list of available colors

Display.desc: Set color used for the red state of temperature values
Display.remark: See `-h color` for the list of available colors

Display.desc: Specify whether to put a space before the unit

enum.default: Use the default behavior of the module
enum.always: Always put a space before the unit
enum.never: Never put a space before the unit
